arts.ttl: Man charged with tragedy chanting at Liverpool’s victory over Manchester City
arts.sum: Eight more arrested on suspicion of same offence Kian Holt will appear before magistrates in January Nine men have been arrested following reports of tragedy chanting at the Premier League match between Liverpool and Manchester City on Sunday. Merseyside Police confirmed the nine, from Greater Manchester, Lancashire and Cheshire, were arrested at Anfield. A 28-year-old man from Prestwich was also arrested on suspicion of common assault. Kian Holt, 19, of Medlar-with-Wesham in Lancashire has been charged with a public order offence under the Football Offence Act 1991, namely tragedy chanting. He is due to appear at Sefton Magistrates Court on 8 January. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Gary Neville aims playful dig at Pep Guardiola after Salford draw struggling Man City in FA Cup
arts.sum: Third round clash will pit City against opponents co-owned by Manchester United’s ‘Class of 92’

arts.ttl: Man charged with tragedy chanting at Liverpool vs Man City
arts.sum: A 19-year-old man has been charged with tragedy chanting during the Premier League match between Liverpool and Manchester City on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Gary Neville can't resist mocking Pep Guardiola as Salford City land huge FA Cup tie
arts.sum: Gary Neville has kept up the "sacked in the morning" chants that were aimed at Pep Guardiola after Salford City were drawn against Manchester City in the FA Cup

arts.ttl: Man United opens FA Cup title defense at Arsenal and Man City to play 'Class of 92'-owned Salford
arts.sum: Among the best were Tamworth, one of only two non-league clubs in the draw, getting to host Tottenham and Manchester City at home to Salford City — the team owned by members of Man United’s famed “Class of 92” such as Ryan Giggs and Gary Neville. Manchester United will start its FA Cup title defense with a trip to Arsenal in a third-round draw containing a host of intriguing English Premier League vs.

arts.ttl: Why Gary Neville and Alan Shearer will be cringing at their own words after seeing Liverpool star v Man City
arts.sum: Liverpool have had some fine players over the years of the Premier League but it’s hard to argue that any have been better – defensively...

arts.ttl: Man City, Man Utd Take Lead in Transfer Race for Barcelona, PSG’s €70M-Rated Target
arts.sum: As 2025 approaches, Viktor Gyökeres is drawing interest from several top European clubs, with Sporting Lisbon reportedly willing to sell their star striker. However, one club seems to be ahead in the race for the Swedish forward. According to L’Équipe’s Loïc Tanzi, Paris Saint-Germain are closely tracking Gyökeres and are planning a move for him […] The post Man City, Man Utd Take Lead in Transfer Race for Barcelona, PSG’s €70M-Rated Target appeared first on PSG Talk.

arts.ttl: Man City are still the Premier League's best in terms of xG but Erling Haaland's conversion rate has been terrible in Pep Guardiola's worst season ever... so is Champions League qualification now in doubt?
arts.sum: According to Opta, City now have only a 4.4 per cent chance of winning the title this season - after being champions in each of the past four campaigns. But where has it all gone so wrong?

arts.ttl: Nottingham Forest fans all saying one thing after drawing rivals Luton Town in the FA Cup
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest have some history when it comes to the FA Cup and tonight the draw has been made for the third round of this...

arts.ttl: Liverpool rub salt in Man City's wounds with new transfer target
arts.sum: The Feyenoord winger was one of the stars in the Champions League match against Man City, in which Feyenoord came back from a 3-0 deficit to draw 3-3. "A few days ago, Hadj-Moussa kept beating one of the most praised defenders in the world, Josko Gvardiol, against Man City", the former Feyenoord player recalls.
